Intellectum Lab

Case Study: Voice AI for Restaurant Order Automation

Client: Restaurant business, New Jersey, USA
Challenge: 40–60 incoming phone orders per evening, calls missed during peak hours, staff diverted from operations to handle phones.

What we built:

A fully automated AI phone assistant that answers calls, takes pickup and delivery orders in natural conversation, and sends confirmed orders to kitchen staff via SMS — with no human involvement in the call.

The process:

The client came with a specific technical preference. We implemented it, tested it in real conditions, and used the results to guide the architecture forward — through three complete iterations over several weeks.

— Version 1 (client's original spec): 68% order completion rate, 1.7s average response latency. Not viable for production.

— Version 2 (streaming AI pipeline): 96.6% completion rate, excellent conversational quality — but 7x over the cost target.

— Version 3 (purpose-built architecture): 100% completion rate, $0.096 per call, 55-second average call duration.

Key engineering decisions:
— Unbundled pipeline: separate best-in-class providers for speech recognition, language model, and voice synthesis — orchestrated through LiveKit
— Non-reasoning language model: faster response, lower cost, no quality loss for structured ordering tasks
— Deterministic flow control: business logic handled by predictable, auditable code — not by the AI model
— Domain-specific speech recognition: custom vocabulary boosting for restaurant menu items
— Prompt caching: 61% cache hit rate, 40% reduction in language model costs

Results:
— Cost per call: $0.096 (target: $0.08–$0.12)
— Order completion rate: 100% in final production testing
— Call duration: 55 seconds average
— System status: live in production since March 2026

We build AI systems that work in production — not systems that work in demos. If you're assessing AI for an operational process, we'd be glad to discuss what a production-grade architecture looks like for your use case.
